text: Age-predicted maximal heart rate in healthy subjects follows the formula HRmax = 211 - 0.64 × age, with a standard error of estimate of 10.8 beats per minute.
Gender does not significantly modify the relationship between age and maximal heart rate.
Physical activity level, VO2max, and BMI do not significantly modify the age-HRmax relationship, meaning the same prediction formula applies regardless of fitness level or body composition.
Previously established HRmax prediction equations (such as 220 - age and 208 - 0.7 × age) underestimate measured HRmax in subjects older than 30 years.
Maximal heart rate in healthy older subjects and women is higher than previously reported in the literature, suggesting prior formulas systematically underestimate HRmax in these groups.

Any age-based HRmax prediction carries an inherent uncertainty of approximately 10.8 beats per minute, meaning individual HRmax can deviate substantially from predicted values and direct measurement is preferable when precision matters.
